records.ety: [L. repercusus, p. p. of repercutere to drive back; pref. re- re- + percutere. See Percussion.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Repercussed (-k?st");p. pr. & vb. n. Repercussing.]

defs: To drive or beat back; hence, to reflect; to reverberate.
quotes: Perceiving all the subjacent country, . . . to repercuss such a light as I could hardly look against.
